MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 3 AND 5. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO MAKE A PRIMA FACIE CASE GIVEN FAILURE TO SHOW HE WAS REPLACED BY SOMEONE YOUNGER. MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MICHAEL CARLIN, AND JOHN CRETAN'S "DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION (THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT)."
APPEARANCE: Ronald J. Hundenski, 510/540-7972 pltf & opposing party in propria persona; Larry Hecimovich, Deputy City Atty 554-3933 for defts & moving parties.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. WRONGFUL TERMINATION CLAIM EVEN BASED ON RETALIATION ALLEGATIONS, IS ONLY ACTIONABLE AGAINST EMPLOYER. Order signed in open court. (301/PJB/PM) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189

AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-02-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ronald Hundenski, 510-540-7972, plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona

No other appearances on this matter. There being no compliance with the tentative ruling rule, no argument was heard. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MICHAEL CARLIN, JOHN CRETAN, MICHAEL DEBELLIS and THERESE MADDEN'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES. Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted as to causes of action 3 and 5. Plaintiff has failed to make a prima facie case given failure to show he was replaced by someone younger. In any event, defendant has shown non discriminatory basis and plaintiff has failed to present any evidence that anything defendant did was on account of plaintiff's age. Prevailing party to prepare form of order. (301/PJB/PM) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189

Off Calendar Aug-25-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Larry Hecimovich of the Office of the City Attorney, 554-3933, appearing for defendant and responding party
Ronald Hundenski, 510-540-7972, plaintiff and moving party appearing in propria persona

The Court made a personal disclosure. No objections were raised, and the matter was argued. PLAINTIFF RONALD HUNDENSKI'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complaint was granted on the following conditions: the amended complaint will add a new cause of action and delete defendants, discovery will be re-opened, defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment can be heard on 45 days notice, and the existing Motion for Summary Judgment will be treated as a Motion for Summary Adjudication. Defendant will prepare a form of order. (301/PJB/CK) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189
